indications.name: Moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease confirmed by endoscopy, colonoscopy, or imaging (CT/MRI enterography)
Failure, intolerance, or contraindication to at least one conventional therapy (corticosteroids such as prednisone, AND at least one immunomodulator: azathioprine, 6-mercaptopurine, or methotrexate)
Negative tuberculosis test (TST or IGRA) within 6 months prior to initiation
Hepatitis B surface antigen screening completed and documented
Patient is not currently pregnant or breastfeeding (or risk/benefit discussed and documented)
indications.clinical_guideline: ECCO Guidelines for Crohn's Disease 2023; ACG Clinical Guideline 2021

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis confirmed by rheumatologist
DAS28 score > 3.2 or CDAI score > 10 documented
Failure or inadequate response to at least two DMARDs including methotrexate
Negative TB test and hepatitis B screening within 6 months

indications.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
HbA1c >= 7.5% documented within the last 3 months
Failure, inadequate response, or documented contraindication to metformin at maximum tolerated dose for at least 3 months
BMI >= 27 kg/m2 documented within the last 12 months
Prescriber is endocrinologist, primary care physician, or internist with documented diabetes management experience
eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2 (renal function documented)
indications.clinical_guideline: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes 2024; AACE/ACE Comprehensive Diabetes Management Algorithm 2023

indications.name: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) — first-line monotherapy
required_criteria: Histologically or cytologically confirmed NSCLC diagnosis with pathology report on file
PD-L1 tumor proportion score (TPS) >= 50% confirmed by FDA-approved diagnostic test (22C3 antibody)
EGFR mutation status documented as negative (wildtype)
ALK rearrangement status documented as negative
ROS1 rearrangement status documented (or testing not indicated due to non-adenocarcinoma histology)
ECOG performance status 0 or 1 documented within 30 days
No active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy within 2 years
No prior systemic chemotherapy for metastatic NSCLC
Prescriber is board-certified oncologist or hematologist/oncologist
indications.clinical_guideline: NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: NSCLC v2.2024; ASCO 2023 Update

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis confirmed by board-certified dermatologist
EASI score >= 16 OR IGA score >= 3 documented within last 6 months
Inadequate response or intolerance to at least two prescription-strength topical corticosteroids of different potency classes used for at least 4 weeks each
Inadequate response, intolerance, or contraindication to at least one systemic immunosuppressant (cyclosporine, methotrexate, azathioprine, or mycophenolate mofetil)
Patient age >= 6 years
Baseline complete blood count and liver function tests documented
indications.clinical_guideline: AAD-NPF Guidelines of Care for Atopic Dermatitis 2023; EADV Guidelines 2022

indications.name: Stroke prevention in non-valvular atrial fibrillation
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of non-valvular atrial fibrillation (ECG or Holter documentation)
CHA2DS2-VASc score >= 2 for men, >= 3 for women — documented calculation on file

One of the following: (a) Documented contraindication to warfarin, (b) Documented INR instability on warfarin with TTR < 60% over 6 months, (c) History of warfarin-related bleeding complication, (d) Patient preference with shared decision-making documented
Renal function documented: CrCl >= 15 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault formula)
No concurrent use of other anticoagulants or antiplatelet combination without documented clinical justification
No mechanical heart valve prosthesis
indications.clinical_guideline: AHA/ACC/HRS Guideline for AF 2023; CHEST Antithrombotic Therapy Guidelines 2022

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis confirmed by dermatologist
BSA involvement >= 10% OR DLQI score >= 10 OR PASI score >= 10 documented
Inadequate response or intolerance to at least two topical therapies (topical corticosteroids, vitamin D analogues, retinoids, or coal tar) used for minimum 3 months
Inadequate response, contraindication, or intolerance to at least one conventional systemic agent (methotrexate, cyclosporine, or acitretin) OR phototherapy (UVB or PUVA) if available
No severe renal impairment (CrCl < 30 mL/min requires dose reduction, must be documented)
Prescriber is dermatologist or rheumatologist (for psoriatic arthritis indication)
indications.clinical_guideline: AAD-NPF Guidelines for Psoriasis 2020; Joint AAD-NPF 2023 Biologic Guidance

indications.name: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF)
required_criteria: Diagnosis of chronic heart failure with LVEF <= 40% documented by echocardiogram, cardiac MRI, or nuclear study within 12 months
NYHA functional class II or III symptoms documented
Current treatment with a stable dose of an ACE inhibitor OR ARB for at least 4 weeks prior to initiation
Adequate blood pressure: systolic BP >= 100 mmHg at time of initiation
eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2 (renal function documented within 3 months)
Potassium < 5.4 mEq/L (documented within 3 months)
No history of angioedema related to previous ACE inhibitor or ARB therapy
36-hour washout period if switching from ACE inhibitor must be documented or planned
Prescriber is cardiologist or heart failure specialist
indications.clinical_guideline: ACC/AHA/HFSA 2022 Heart Failure Guideline; ESC 2021 Heart Failure Guidelines

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease — biologic-experienced
required_criteria: Confirmed moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease by endoscopy or imaging
Failure, loss of response, or intolerance to at least one TNF-alpha inhibitor (adalimumab, infliximab, or certolizumab pegol)
Failure or inadequate response to conventional therapies (corticosteroids and immunomodulators)
Negative TB test and hepatitis B screening within 6 months
No active serious infection at time of initiation
Prescriber is gastroenterologist
indications.clinical_guideline: ECCO Guidelines 2023; ACG 2021 Biologic Therapy Guidance

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis by endoscopy
Mayo endoscopic score >= 2 documented
Failure, intolerance, or contraindication to at least one TNF-alpha inhibitor (infliximab or adalimumab)
Adequate response to conventional therapies assessed (mesalamine, corticosteroids, azathioprine)
Negative TB test and hepatitis B screening
No current active, serious, or chronic infection
Absolute lymphocyte count >= 750 cells/mm3
Hemoglobin >= 8 g/dL
Not recommended in patients > 65 years, current or past smokers, or those with cardiovascular risk factors without clear benefit/risk discussion documented
Prescriber is gastroenterologist
indications.clinical_guideline: ACG 2022 UC Guidelines; ECCO UC Consensus 2022; FDA REMS requirement for JAK inhibitors

indications.name: Chronic insomnia disorder
required_criteria: Diagnosis of chronic insomnia disorder (difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep >= 3 nights/week for >= 3 months)
Documented failure of cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) or documented barrier to access with clinical justification
Failure or inadequate response to at least two first-line pharmacologic agents: zolpidem, eszopiclone, or zaleplon
No concurrent use of other CNS depressants without documented clinical justification
Sleep study (polysomnography) completed to rule out obstructive sleep apnea if clinically indicated
No narcolepsy diagnosis
Prescriber is sleep medicine specialist, psychiatrist, or primary care physician with documented sleep medicine training
indications.clinical_guideline: AASM Clinical Practice Guideline for Insomnia 2023; ACP Insomnia Management Guidelines 2021

indications.name: Chronic kidney disease to reduce eGFR decline and cardiovascular events
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of chronic kidney disease (CKD) stage 3a or higher
eGFR 25–75 mL/min/1.73m2 documented within last 3 months
UACR >= 200 mg/g documented (or Type 2 diabetes diagnosis)
Absence of type 1 diabetes
No current dialysis or kidney transplant
eGFR >= 25 mL/min/1.73m2 at time of initiation
Prescriber is nephrologist, endocrinologist, or primary care physician with CKD management experience
indications.clinical_guideline: KDIGO 2022 CKD Guidelines; ADA 2024 Standards of Care; ACC/AHA SGLT2i recommendations

indications.name: Relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS)
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis per McDonald Criteria 2017, confirmed by neurologist
MRI evidence of active disease (T1 gadolinium-enhancing lesions OR new/enlarging T2 lesions) within last 12 months

Failure or inadequate response to at least one first-line disease-modifying therapy (interferon beta-1a, interferon beta-1b, glatiramer acetate, dimethyl fumarate) for minimum 6 months, OR highly active disease warranting escalation therapy
JC virus antibody index documented prior to initiation (required for PML risk stratification)
Enrolled in TOUCH Prescribing Program (FDA REMS requirement — documented enrollment required)
No concurrent use of other immunosuppressive therapies
Baseline MRI within 3 months prior to first infusion
Prescriber is board-certified neurologist enrolled in TOUCH program
indications.clinical_guideline: AAN Practice Guidelines for MS 2022; ECTRIMS/EAN MS Treatment Guidelines 2021
